questions: The continuous effects in continuous.tsx don't use effectFrames at all — but custom/dynamic continuous effects might. Should the canonical helper pass effectFrames=durationInFrames for continuous effects (giving custom effects the info) or effectFrames=undefined (cleaner contract, but breaks any custom continuous effect that relies on effectFrames)?

EffectLayerSequence is only used for continuous effect layers (effect-layer clipType). Should it remain its own component (handling the time-range gating via useCurrentFrame), or should we fold its time-gating logic into the canonical helper too?

The wrapWithClipEffects function applies effects in a specific order: continuous → entrance → exit (inner to outer). Is this the correct nesting order, or should entrance be innermost (so it runs first in the visual stack)?

success_criteria.criterion: A single canonical wrapSingleEffect function exists in effects/index.tsx that all three code paths (wrapWithClipEffects, EffectLayerSequence, makePreviewComposition) use to wrap content with an effect component
effectFrames contract is consistent: entrance/exit get computed effectFrames, continuous gets a defined value (either undefined or durationInFrames, per decision on Q1)
PREVIEW_FPS constant is removed; inline 30 used as fallback
All existing tests in src/tools/video-editor/effects/ pass
useEffectRegistry useEffect has a stale guard to prevent race conditions from rapid dependency changes
console.log('[EffectLookup]...') debug logging removed from lookupEffect
EffectLayerSequence.tsx no longer directly imports EffectErrorBoundary or validateAndCoerceParams
EffectCreatorPanel.tsx no longer directly imports EffectErrorBoundary
Each file touched stays under ~300 lines
wrapSingleEffect has at least one unit test covering the happy path and the not-found path
No regressions in full video-editor test suite (npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/)
Visual smoke test: preview in EffectCreatorPanel still renders correctly for entrance, exit, and continuous effects
Visual smoke test: timeline render with entrance + continuous + exit effects on a clip renders identically to before

assumptions: The effect application order (continuous innermost → entrance → exit outermost) in wrapWithClipEffects is intentional and correct — will preserve it.

Built-in continuous effects ignoring effectFrames is by design (they use durationInFrames for animation). Custom continuous effects may or may not use it, but we'll default to passing effectFrames=undefined for continuous and document that custom continuous effects should use durationInFrames.
EffectLayerSequence should remain its own component (it has unique time-range gating logic with useCurrentFrame) — only the inner effect wrapping will be delegated to the canonical helper.
The replaceEffectRegistry call during render in useEffectRegistry is an intentional pattern to keep the global singleton in sync — not a bug to fix.

getSchema is O(1) and does not need per-frame caching — the audit concern about 'registry.getSchema called multiple times per frame' is a non-issue for built-in effects (returns undefined quickly) and only marginally relevant for dynamic effects.
